q4.accuracy_comparison_reasoning: Decreasing the depth reduces overfitting as the model does not grow too deep, so training accuracy decreases but test accuracy improves
q4.plot_identification_reasoning: Root node gives the best attribute for split, so the first split feature is most important
q4.verification_reasoning: Feature importance values show gender has the highest contribution

observation_accuracy.answer: The training and test accuracy decrease on adding noise
observation_accuracy.reasoning: The noise changes labels, so the model learns wrong patterns, leading to lower accuracy

observation_depth.answer: The depth increases on adding noise
observation_depth.reasoning: The noise adds complexity, so the model grows deeper to fit the noisy data

observation_features.answer: The features are completely different on adding noise
observation_features.reasoning: Noise affects feature-label relation, so important features can shift

q9_1.answer: As the max_depth increases, the training accuracy increases, but the test accuracy decreases after depth 4
q9_1.reasoning: As the max_depth increases, the model starts fitting noise and therefore overfits to the training data

q9_2.reasoning: Depth 3 gives the highest test accuracy, so it is the best choice for deployment as it generalizes better to unseen data

q9_3.answer: The gap increases significantly from depth 3 to depth 10
q9_3.reasoning: As the max_depth increases, the model starts overfitting to the training data, so the training accuracy increases while the test accuracy decreases, leading to a larger gap between them. This indicates that the model is not generalizing well to unseen data at higher depths.
